fact: January 25 is exactly 35 days before the first day of spring in the Northern Hemisphere - that's exactly 5 weeks of winter left to go!
In Antarctica, the sun never sets during this time of year! It's summer there, so penguins are experiencing 24 hours of daylight.
Tree sap is typically frozen solid in cold climates today, which means if you tap a maple tree, nothing comes out - you have to wait until March for syrup season!
Groundhogs are usually fast asleep underground during this time, curled up in balls with their heart rate slowed to just 5 beats per minute instead of the normal 80!
In the Northern Hemisphere, sunrise typically happens about 1 minute and 30 seconds earlier than the day before - the days are getting longer fast!
Today falls in the middle of Aquarius season, making it one of the most 'Aquarius-y' days of the year for anyone who follows zodiac signs.
Polar bears in the Arctic are usually hunting on thick sea ice right now - the ice is typically at its strongest and thickest point of the entire year.
January 25 is the 25th day of the year, which means there are exactly 340 days left until next New Year's Eve (or 341 in leap years).
Desert foxes in cold regions have typically grown their fluffiest winter coats by now - their fur is about twice as thick as it was in summer to trap warm air!
The Earth is actually closer to the sun today than it will be in July, but the Northern Hemisphere is tilted away, which is why winter feels so cold despite being closer to our star.
Hibernating bats are usually hanging upside down in caves right now, breathing only once every few minutes and living off stored body fat.
